title: [Comb] Remove more idempotent operands
body:
I'm unsure if this is a good way to go about this, but I wanted to try my hand at #6840. I'm unsure if this is a good way to go about the problem, any feedback would be greatly appreciated.

The example provided by @uenoku in #6840 is
```
hw.module @Baz(in %cond : i2, in %value : i2, out o1: i2, out o2: i2) {
  %0 = comb.and bin %cond, %value : i2
  %1 = comb.and bin %0, %value : i2
  hw.output %0, %1: i2, i2
}
```
which canonicalizes to
```
$ ./bin/circt-opt --canonicalize bin/baz.mlir
module {
  hw.module @Baz(in %cond : i2, in %value : i2, out o1 : i2, out o2 : i2) {
    %0 = comb.and bin %cond, %value : i2
    %1 = comb.and bin %0, %value : i2
    hw.output %0, %1 : i2, i2
  }
}
```
instead of
```
module {
  hw.module @Baz(in %cond : i2, in %value : i2, out o1 : i2, out o2 : i2) {
    %0 = comb.and bin %cond, %value : i2
    hw.output %0, %0 : i2, i2
  }
}
```
Currently `template <typename Op> bool canonicalizeIdempotentInputs(Op op, PatternRewriter &rewriter)` makes sure that a value can appear _maximum one time_ in `op`'s operands. This patch makes `template <typename Op> bool canonicalizeIdempotentInputs(Op op, PatternRewriter &rewriter)` consider whether any of `op`s operands are themselves defined by an `Op`, whose operands we can omit from `op`'s operands.

In the example above, that means

1. `%1` is a `comb.and` whose operands are `%0`, `%value`
2. `%0` is an `comb.and`, matching `%1`s operation
3. `%value` and be removed from `%1`, since input `0` of `%1` is a `comb.and` with a `%value` operand
